name: Sierra Leone
experiences: Tiwai Island primates and canoe wildlife
avoid: May to October is far wetter, so peninsula roads, Bunce and Banana boat crossings, and Tiwai forest walks all get slower and muddier.
Cash is still essential for poda-podas, okadas, community-run beach entries, island boats, and many smaller guesthouses outside Freetown.
Domestic flights inside Sierra Leone are limited and often charter-led, so most realistic itineraries still hinge on long road transfers plus boat legs.
stopoverNote: Lungi Airport sits across the estuary from Freetown, so most travelers start with a Sea Bird Express water-taxi or road-and-ferry transfer into Aberdeen before building a peninsula loop; the coastal islands are easiest to combine with Freetown, while Tiwai needs a committed southeast road leg.

cities.name: Freetown
cities.notes: Best base for airport logistics, city history, Tacugama, River No. 2, Lumley dining, and onward boats to Bunce and the Banana Islands.

cities.name: Banana Islands
cities.notes: Rustic Dublin and Ricketts islands for orange-sand beaches, fishing-village life, simple guesthouses, and boat-led snorkeling or diving.

cities.name: Tiwai Island
cities.notes: Community-run rainforest sanctuary on the Moa River with high primate density, canoe access, and simple eco-lodge accommodation.

cities.name: Bunce Island
cities.notes: Moving estuary day trip to one of West Africa's most important slave-fort ruins, usually reached by speedboat from Cockle Bay with a guide.

itinerary.sim: Africell is the safest one-SIM choice for Freetown, the peninsula and most main-road sections toward Bo and Tiwai; Orange is fine in town but patchier inland. Budget roughly ₹370–₹1,110 for a starter SIM or data top-up with enough maps and WhatsApp coverage for a week.

days.theme: Handle the classic Sierra Leone arrival, then ease into the capital with low-stress history stops and a Lumley-side dinner.

activities.name: Sea Bird Express water-taxi from Lungi Airport to Aberdeen
Taxi from Aberdeen jetty to Lumley, Aberdeen or Hill Station hotels
National Museum of Sierra Leone donation or entry contribution
Cotton Tree and St. George's Cathedral walk
Okada or taxi hops between central Freetown, Aberdeen and Lumley

days.theme: Use a full peninsula day for Sierra Leone's easiest wildlife stop and its most famous community beach.

activities.name: Tacugama Chimpanzee Sanctuary guided foreign visitor tour
Driver, taxi or hotel car from Freetown to Tacugama and onward to River No. 2 Beach
River No. 2 Beach community entry fee
Beach chair, shade or simple changing-room setup at River No. 2
Cold drink or coconut budget along the peninsula beaches

days.theme: Keep the third day flexible for city texture, craft markets and seafront time before the island legs begin.

activities.name: Big Market or Aberdeen craft-market browsing
Lumley Beach promenade and beach-club time
Old Fourah Bay College viewpoint or Hill Station drive
Shared taxi or okada rides around Lumley, Wilberforce and Congo Cross
Water, snacks and cash top-up buffer before Bunce or Tiwai departures

Bunce Island.name: Bunce Island
Bunce Island.note: Almost everyone does Bunce as a guided day trip from Freetown; overnighting on nearby Tasso Island is the only practical extension.

days.theme: Make space for Sierra Leone's most important historical excursion, pairing the estuary boat ride with a guide-led fort walk.

activities.name: Speedboat from Cockle Bay or Aqua Sports Club to Bunce Island return
Bunce Island foreign visitor entry and guiding fee
Guided walk through the slave yards, bastions and fort ruins
Tasso Island landing and community beach lunch stop
Taxi or okada to and from the Freetown jetty

days.theme: Commit to the long southeastern transfer, then settle into one of Sierra Leone's best community-run wildlife sanctuaries.

activities.name: Poda-poda, shared car or arranged driver from Freetown toward Bo-Barri Chiefdom and Tiwai jetty
Final okada or short local transfer to the river crossing point
Canoe or small boat transfer onto Tiwai Island
Tiwai Island Wildlife Sanctuary foreign day entry
Sunset birding or riverside walk after check-in

days.theme: Use the cool morning for primates and forest walks, then add canoe wildlife or community time while the light stays soft.

activities.name: Guided primate and rainforest walk on Tiwai trails
Moa River canoe wildlife trip for birds and possible pygmy hippo habitat viewing
Community visit in one of Tiwai's host villages
Second sanctuary day fee if staying overnight again
Simple lantern, charging or bottled-water budget at camp

days.theme: Return to the peninsula for a slower finish built around Kent boats, village life and a simple beach stay on Dublin Island.

activities.name: Road transfer or poda-poda back west toward Kent from the Bo or Freetown side
Community boat or accommodation-arranged boat from Kent to Dublin, Banana Islands
Dublin village walk including the old cannons and church area
Big Sand Beach swim and sunset time
Stone causeway walk from Dublin to Ricketts

days.theme: Keep the final island day loose with water activities, lobster on Ricketts and an easy boat back when you're ready.

activities.name: Kayak, pedalo or snorkeling arranged through Bafa Resort or Daltons
Boat hop around Ricketts or nearby coves
Fresh lobster or seafood lunch on Ricketts
Return boat from Dublin to Kent
Taxi or shared car from Kent back to Freetown

connections.method: Speedboat day trips from Cockle Bay or Aqua Sports Club are the normal Bunce route; some operators fold in Tasso Island for lunch.
Return to Freetown first, then continue southeast by arranged driver, shared car or a chain of poda-podas; domestic flights are limited, so Tiwai is usually still a road-plus-canoe journey.
Backtrack west by road to the peninsula, then switch to the local Kent boat or an accommodation-arranged charter into Dublin Island.
Boat back to Kent, then a shared car, taxi or poda-poda up the peninsula into Lumley or Aberdeen.

extras: November to April is the best all-round window: Bunce and Banana crossings are more reliable, River No. 2 stays easier to enjoy, and Tiwai trails are far less waterlogged.
Book Bunce boats and Tacugama tours ahead in peak dry-season weeks because both depend on guides, tide timing, or vehicle coordination.
Carry small notes for okadas, poda-podas, beach fees, and island snacks; cards only work reliably in Freetown's better hotels and restaurants.
Tiwai and Banana are both low-infrastructure stops, so pack a torch, power bank and a little patience for patchy charging and slower meal service.
If you are short on time, treat Tiwai as the one place worth paying more for a private transfer; it saves a lot of friction versus piecing together multiple public legs.
Bunce is emotionally heavy and mostly guide-dependent, so many travelers prefer putting a lighter beach or island day either before or after it.
